summary: The effect of thickness on gypsum-casting properties is complex; thinning may improve ergonomic handling without significantly altering expansion rates but can lead to more controlled volume changes during setting, which helps minimize cracking risks associated with uneven internal pressure buildup typical at phase interfaces under hydration-induced stresses. Thinner investments offer advantages like reduced material waste and easier manipulation, contributing positively towards predictable outcomes despite minor adjustments needed in procedural techniques such as timing and technique refinement based on specific application requirements.
